summary: The revision correctly closes the prior gap. claude_test.go is now in B1's scope (plan step 4 + Changed files + AC L257 + provenance). The class-(a)/class-(b) split is verified CORRECT against the actual source. Class (a) tests are genuinely driven by the REAL wantedHooks(): TestRunClaude_FreshSetup (L47-48, via RunClaude->ensureSettings->ensureSettingsWith(...,wantedHooks())) hard-asserts the PreToolUse pre-complete entry PRESENT after fresh setup -> must invert to absent; TestWantedHooks_HasPreCompletePreToolUse (L227-246) calls wantedHooks() directly and asserts exactly-one PreToolUse Bash entry -> correctly DELETED wholesale; TestRunClaude_CleansStalePreToolUse (L768-769) asserts the pre-complete entry PRESENT after cleanup via RunClaude -> must invert to absent. All three flip once wantedHooks() drops the PreToolUse key (claude.go L324-335) and are correctly bucketed as INVERT/DELETE. Class (b) tests are genuinely independent: wantedWithPreComplete() (L283-287) does `wanted := wantedHooks(); wanted["PreToolUse"] = []map[string]any{preCompleteWantedEntry()}` — it OVERWRITES the PreToolUse key with a SYNTHETIC entry, so after R1 strips the real key the injected entry is still present, keeping TestRemoveStaleMindspecEntries_WantedEntrySurvives (L406), TestEnsureSettings_MergePathInstallsPreCompleteEntry (L562), _UserPreToolUseEntrySurvives (L608), _RerunIdempotent (L655) GREEN; TestMergeWantedEntry_AppendsAlongsideUserEntry (L354) drives the merge directly off preCompleteWantedEntry() with no wantedHooks() dependency. These are correctly KEPT. Completeness verified by grepping pre-complete/PreToolUse/hooksContainCommand: the ONLY assertions that flip after the wantedHooks() change are the three class-(a) tests; the remaining PreToolUse-touching tests (_RemovesLegacyEntries L426, _PreservesUserEntries L444, _NoOpWhenNothingStale L467) pass the real wantedHooks() but only exercise legacy/user entries (worktree-file/worktree-bash/instruct, user hooks) which stay stale-removed/preserved identically with or without the pre-complete key — they do NOT flip. Created-item counts (FreshSetup L24=13, Idempotent L100/101=13) track FILES not hook entries, so dropping an in-settings hook entry leaves them unchanged (plan flags this). No missed assertion. After the rework, `go test -run 'TestSetup|TestClaude' ./internal/setup/` would PASS GREEN with the entry absent.
